title: cistus berthelotianus
intro: Cistus berthelotianus, also known as the Berthelot's rockrose, is a species of flowering plant in the family Cistaceae. It is a shrub-like perennial plant native to the island of Tenerife in the Canary Islands, known for its medicinal properties and health benefits. Cistus berthelotianus contains flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have been found to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it useful for treating wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory issues. The plant can be used in various herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits, such as improving cardiovascular health and boosting the immune system.
